notes.upgrade_1_terrain_constrained_paths: DESERT path perpendicular offset increased from 12% to 16% of segment length for wider/softer curves. MOUNTAIN paths now use a cubic bezier (3 control points) instead of quadratic — two internal kink control points at 35% and 65% along the straight line, offset opposite/same sign respectively, producing a slight angular switchback feel. STEPS reduced from 20 to 14 for MOUNTAIN for a mildly faceted look consistent with rocky terrain. Midpoint biome sampling added: nearest anchor lookup at (ax+bx)/2, (ay+by)/2; if midpoint biome differs from both endpoints it becomes the dominant biome for path style selection.
notes.upgrade_2_landmark_clustering: Added Phase 1 cluster pass before the existing per-tile straggler pass. 8 cluster anchors per scene placed deterministically from CLUSTER_SEED (= SEED ^ 0xC1A57E2). Each anchor's biome is sampled from nearest location anchor. Each cluster spawns 2–4 child landmarks within 40 px radius using polar coordinates from CLUSTER_SEED sub-seeds. WATER and UNKNOWN biome clusters are skipped. Phase 2 (original per-tile pass) still runs as the straggler layer, consuming whatever remains of the 80-landmark cap. Total count <= 80 guaranteed.
notes.upgrade_3_node_anchoring: Added a dedicated nodeBaseG = this.add.graphics() rendered at depth 0 before the node circles (depth 1). Iterates all locations, draws drop-shadow ellipse (rgba(0,0,0,0.18), 36×12 px, offset y+2) then biome-matched base: DESERT=tan ellipse (32×10, alpha 0.5), MOUNTAIN=3 overlapping gray ellipses at offsets (-4,4)/(+4,5)/(0,3) for irregular rocky look, GRASSLAND=green ellipse (32×10, alpha 0.45), WATER=light blue ellipse + tiny tan dock rectangle below, ROCK=dark gray ellipse (32×10, alpha 0.5), URBAN=light gray strokeRect outline.
notes.upgrade_4_path_integration: All stroke alphas reduced ~10 pp: DESERT 0.60→0.50, GRASSLAND 0.65→0.55, MOUNTAIN 0.75→0.65, default 0.60→0.50. Locked-path multiplier reduced from 0.35 to 0.25 of post-reduction alpha. Soft endpoint: last 3 bezier segments (i >= STEPS-2) drawn with strokeWidth-1 (minimum 1) at 85% alpha — implemented by breaking and restarting the path at that segment. Applies to both quadratic (non-mountain) and cubic (mountain) draw paths.
notes.upgrade_5_depth_consistency: All depth assignments now explicit: _terrainContainer.setDepth(-100) added (was relying on creation order), _pathContainer.setDepth(-50) kept, _landmarkContainer.setDepth(-25) ADDED (previously default 0), nodeBaseG.setDepth(0), node circle/icon/label/stars/quest indicators all .setDepth(1), vignette vg.setDepth(1000). Neighborhood home marker also .setDepth(1).
notes.upgrade_6_landmark_variation: drawLandmark() helper extracts rotation (±15° via rand2, deterministic per tx/ty) and scale (0.85–1.15× via rand2) per landmark. Rotation applied via manual cosR/sinR transform on component (dx,dy) offsets — rp() helper rotates+scales a point. Cactus micro-variation: arm count 1 or 2 per rand2(SEED^0x7777). Rock formation triangles rotated. Ridge lines scaled. Urban structures rotated. Grassland scrub uses sc-scaled circle radii. All rotation/scale seeds are distinct sub-seeds of SEED/CLUSTER_SEED XOR'd with per-landmark tx/ty.
notes.upgrade_7_micro_contrast: Cactus fill alpha bumped to 1.0 (was implicit 1.0 per fillStyle spec, made explicit). Desert rock formation grays: mid-triangle darkened 0x8A8070→0x6A6050 for more contrast. Mountain ridge: stroke color darkened 0x5A5555→0x4A4545, alpha 0.85→0.95. Rock biome boulder: fill darkened 0x7A7265→0x6A6255. Grassland scrub: alpha 0.85→0.95. Urban structure: lineStyle alpha 0.90→1.0. The terrain palette itself is unchanged per guardrails.
notes.vignette: Trapezoid-edge implementation chosen over radial gradient. Phaser 3 Graphics.fillGradientStyle provides a 4-corner linear gradient per rect but has no native radial function. A radial gradient would require WebGL shaders or a RenderTexture pass — both disproportionate for a cosmetic vignette. Four gradient rects cover TOP/BOTTOM/LEFT/RIGHT edges, each 22% of the shorter viewport dimension deep, fading from ALPHA_OUTER=0.30 at the edge to ALPHA_INNER=0 at the center. Corners are double-covered (where two edge rects overlap), which naturally darkens the corners more — the desired visual behavior. vg.setDepth(1000), vg.setData('occlusionRole','safe'). Graphics objects have no interactive region by default so input passes through to nodes below.
notes.zone_circle_deletion: The original codebase (verified by reading WorldMapScene.js at HEAD commit 3775a32) had NO separate large zone-circle around nodes. The only circle draw was `this.add.circle(x, y, 22, color, unlocked ? 1 : 0.4)` which is the actual interactive node circle (r=22 px). There were no additional fillCircle rings or large translucent zone overlays to delete. The spec instruction to delete them was precautionary. The 22px node circle is retained as it is the interactive hit target for `pointerdown`.
notes.label_opacity: Inactive node labels now call nameLabel.setAlpha(0.7). Active (unlocked) labels remain at default 1.0 alpha. No glow added to active labels as the existing stroke: '#f5e6c8' strokeThickness:2 already provides adequate contrast.
notes.click_handler_verification: Three mental traces: (1) desert_foraging: unlocked=true → circle.on('pointerdown', () => this._selectLocation(loc)) → _selectLocation checks scene registry, sets gameState.player.scene='DesertForagingScene', saveGame(), fadeOut, scene.start('DesertForagingScene'). Circle is still setInteractive({useHandCursor:true}). Handler untouched. (2) copper_mine: unlocked state depends on reputation/quest; if locked → circle.setInteractive({useHandCursor:false}) → circle.on('pointerdown', () => this._showLockHint(loc,state)). Handler untouched. (3) salt_river: same as copper_mine pattern. All click/hover handlers are in the second for-loop over locations which is structurally unchanged — only the node circle now has .setDepth(1) added.
notes.biome_base_tuning_notes: UNKNOWN biome (Chinese/Malay regions) gets a neutral faded ellipse (0x706050, alpha 0.35) which is visually unobtrusive but may look slightly flat against the dark UNKNOWN terrain tile. If those regions are later given explicit biomes the base will auto-update. DESERT base (0xB49664) may appear slightly warm against the parchment background when the node is at the right edge of the map — could consider reducing alpha to 0.4 if it clashes. MOUNTAIN three-ellipse base looks appropriately irregular for the current dataset.
notes.performance_note: Polish additions cost: nodeBaseG is ~3 fillEllipse + 0-3 extra ops per node (3 locations * ~5 ops = ~15 Graphics calls, negligible). Landmark drawLandmark() adds 2 trig calls (cos/sin) and 2 rand2 calls per landmark vs original — approximately +15 μs per landmark at 80 total = ~1.2 ms overhead. Cluster phase adds 8*3 rand2 calls + ~24 child landmark draws = O(80) same as before since cap is shared. Vignette adds 4 fillGradientStyle + 4 fillRect calls — <0.1 ms. Total polish overhead estimated at <2 ms on top of existing terrain ~15-25 ms budget.
notes.git_diff_summary: Changed blocks in WorldMapScene.js: (a) create() — added nodeBaseG loop (lines ~182-249), added .setDepth(1) to all node display objects, added setAlpha(0.7) to inactive labels, added _renderVignette() call; (b) new _renderVignette() method (~390-417); (c) _renderPathLayer() — rewritten with midpoint biome sampling, biome-conditional offset%, cubic bezier for MOUNTAIN with kink control points, reduced alphas, soft endpoints; (d) _renderTerrainLayer() — added container.setDepth(-100); (e) _renderLandmarkLayer() — added container.setDepth(-25), replaced per-biome draw blocks with drawLandmark() helper that applies rotation/scale, added Phase 1 cluster pass before Phase 2 straggler pass, updated all contrast values per #7.
